title.en: What Is 802.15.4? Low-Rate WPANs, Topologies and Architecture
title.zh: 什么是 802.15.4？低速 WPAN、拓扑与架构

blocks.en: IEEE Std 802.15.4-2024 defines the bottom two layers — the physical layer (PHY, the radio) and the medium access control sublayer (MAC, the rules for sharing the air) — of a low-rate wireless personal area network, abbreviated LR-WPAN. Its scope is wireless connectivity for fixed, portable, and moving devices that have no battery or only a very limited power budget, and some of its PHYs add precision ranging accurate to about one meter (Clause 1.1). The purpose is deliberately narrow: ultra-low complexity, ultra-low cost, ultra-low power, and a low data rate, aimed squarely at the communication needs of the Internet of Things (Clause 1.2). This is the radio-and-MAC foundation that popular stacks such as Zigbee and Thread build on top of.
blocks.zh: IEEE Std 802.15.4-2024 定义了低速无线个域网（缩写 LR-WPAN）最底下的两层——物理层（PHY，即无线电）与介质访问控制子层（MAC，即共享空口的规则）。它的范围是为固定、便携和移动设备提供无线连接，这些设备没有电池或只有非常有限的功耗预算；其中部分 PHY 还增加了精度约一米的精确测距能力（第 1.1 条）。它的目的非常聚焦：极低复杂度、极低成本、极低功耗与低数据速率，正对准物联网（IoT）的通信需求（第 1.2 条）。这正是 Zigbee、Thread 等常见协议栈所依赖的无线电与 MAC 基础。

blocks.en: Word usage matters in standards (Clause 1.3): "shall" means a mandatory requirement, "should" means recommended, "may" means permitted, and "can" means able to. Reading these words precisely is the difference between what a conforming device must do and what it merely could do.
blocks.zh: 标准里的用词很讲究（第 1.3 条）："shall（应）"表示强制要求，"should（宜）"表示推荐，"may（可）"表示允许，"can（能）"表示具备能力。精确理解这些词，决定了你能区分一致性设备"必须"做什么与它"仅仅可以"做什么。

blocks.en: Clause 3 supplies the shared vocabulary. Key definitions (Clause 3.1) include: a coordinator (any device able to provide coordinator functions), association (the service that establishes a device's membership in a network), a frame (the bits a MAC entity transmits together in time), a transaction (the related, consecutive frames exchanged for one successful command or data delivery), and channel hopping (periodically switching channel using a sequence both ends know). A long list of acronyms (Clause 3.2) defines shorthand you will meet throughout the standard — for example LR-WPAN itself, MAC, PHY, MPDU (MAC protocol data unit), IE (information element), GTS (guaranteed timeslot), and CSMA-CA (carrier sense multiple access with collision avoidance).
blocks.zh: 第 3 条提供了共享词汇。关键定义（第 3.1 条）包括：协调器（coordinator，任何能提供协调器功能的设备）、关联（association，确立设备入网成员身份的服务）、帧（frame，MAC 实体在时间上一并发送的比特）、事务（transaction，为一次成功的命令或数据投递而交换的一组连续相关帧），以及信道跳变（channel hopping，按收发双方共知的序列周期性切换信道）。一长串缩略语（第 3.2 条）定义了贯穿全标准的简写——例如 LR-WPAN 本身、MAC、PHY、MPDU（MAC 协议数据单元）、IE（信息元素）、GTS（保证时隙）以及 CSMA-CA（带冲突避免的载波侦听多址）。

blocks.en: An LR-WPAN is a simple, low-cost network for connectivity where power is limited and throughput needs are relaxed; its objectives are easy installation, reliable transfer, very low cost, and reasonable battery life behind one simple, flexible protocol (Clause 5.1). The standard defines one MAC plus multiple PHYs across several frequency bands. The most basic building block is the device: a single radio interface implementing an 802.15.4 MAC and PHY. Two or more devices communicating on the same physical channel form a WPAN (Clause 5.3).
blocks.zh: LR-WPAN 是一种简单、低成本的网络，用于在功耗受限、吞吐要求宽松的场景下提供连接；其目标是安装便捷、传输可靠、成本极低、电池寿命合理，并以一套简单灵活的协议实现（第 5.1 条）。标准定义了一套 MAC 以及覆盖多个频段的多种 PHY。最基本的组成单元是设备（device）：一个实现了 802.15.4 MAC 与 PHY 的单一无线电接口。两个或多个在同一物理信道上通信的设备就构成一个 WPAN（第 5.3 条）。

blocks.en: Although 802.15.4 targets many uses, certain spaces have unique needs that drove specific additions (Clause 5.2.1): smart utility networks (SUN), rail communications and control (RCC), television white space (TVWS), and low-energy critical-infrastructure monitoring (LECIM). Three more are described directly here: radio frequency identification (RFID), where transmit-only tags periodically broadcast an ID for asset and inventory tracking (Clause 5.2.2); medical body area network (MBAN) services, which use spectrum on a secondary basis and must vacate bands when a primary user appears (Clause 5.2.3); and the China medical band (CMB), specific bands approved by China's MIIT for medical, non-voice use (Clause 5.2.4).
blocks.zh: 尽管 802.15.4 面向众多用途，但某些应用空间因有独特需求而促成了专门的补充（第 5.2.1 条）：智能公用网络（SUN）、铁路通信与控制（RCC）、电视白频段（TVWS），以及低能耗关键基础设施监测（LECIM）。另有三类在此直接描述：射频识别（RFID），即只发射的标签周期性广播 ID，用于资产与库存追踪（第 5.2.2 条）；医疗体域网（MBAN）服务，以次要身份使用频谱，当主用户出现时必须让出频段（第 5.2.3 条）；以及中国医疗频段（CMB），即中国工信部批准用于医疗、非语音用途的特定频段（第 5.2.4 条）。

blocks.en: An LR-WPAN runs in one of two topologies (Clause 5.4.1). In the star topology, every device talks to a single central controller called the PAN coordinator — the primary controller of the personal area network — which is often mains powered while leaf devices run on battery. Every device has a unique extended address and may also be assigned a short address during association. In the peer-to-peer topology, any device can talk directly to any other in range; it still has a PAN coordinator, but it supports richer formations such as mesh and multi-hop routing (those routing functions live in a higher layer, outside this standard). Each PAN picks a unique PAN ID so short addresses stay unambiguous.
blocks.zh: LR-WPAN 以两种拓扑之一运行（第 5.4.1 条）。在星形拓扑中，每个设备都与单一的中心控制器通信，该控制器称为 PAN 协调器——个域网的主控制器——它通常由市电供电，而叶子设备则靠电池运行。每个设备都有唯一的扩展地址，并可在关联过程中被分配一个短地址。在对等（peer-to-peer）拓扑中，任意设备都可与其射程内的任意其他设备直接通信；它同样有一个 PAN 协调器，但支持更丰富的组网形态，例如网状网与多跳路由（这些路由功能位于更高层，不属于本标准）。每个 PAN 选取一个唯一的 PAN ID，使短地址不会产生歧义。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 420 180" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><text x="70" y="16" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">Star</text><circle cx="70" cy="90" r="16"/><text x="70" y="94" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">PAN</text><circle cx="70" cy="40" r="9"/><circle cx="30" cy="110" r="9"/><circle cx="110" cy="110" r="9"/><circle cx="40" cy="70" r="9"/><circle cx="100" cy="70" r="9"/><line x1="70" y1="74" x2="70" y2="49"/><line x1="58" y1="99" x2="37" y2="107"/><line x1="82" y1="99" x2="103" y2="107"/><line x1="57" y1="82" x2="46" y2="75"/><line x1="83" y1="82" x2="94" y2="75"/><text x="310" y="16" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">Peer-to-peer / cluster tree</text><circle cx="310" cy="45" r="15"/><text x="310" y="49" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">PAN</text><circle cx="250" cy="100" r="10"/><circle cx="370" cy="100" r="10"/><circle cx="220" cy="150" r="9"/><circle cx="280" cy="150" r="9"/><circle cx="350" cy="150" r="9"/><circle cx="395" cy="150" r="9"/><line x1="299" y1="55" x2="258" y2="92"/><line x1="321" y1="55" x2="362" y2="92"/><line x1="244" y1="109" x2="225" y2="142"/><line x1="256" y1="109" x2="275" y2="142"/><line x1="364" y1="109" x2="352" y2="142"/><line x1="376" y1="109" x2="392" y2="142"/></svg>

caption.en: Star (left): all devices reach one PAN coordinator. Peer-to-peer / cluster tree (right): intermediate devices relay and synchronize, with one overall PAN coordinator.
caption.zh: 星形（左）：所有设备都连到一个 PAN 协调器。对等／簇树（右）：中间设备负责中继与同步，全网仅有一个总的 PAN 协调器。

blocks.en: How does a network come into being? In star network formation (Clause 5.4.2), once a device is activated it can start its own network and become the PAN coordinator by choosing a PAN ID not already in use within radio range, then admitting other devices. In peer-to-peer network formation (Clause 5.4.3), one device is nominated PAN coordinator — for instance the first to transmit on the channel. The cluster tree is a special peer-to-peer case: the PAN coordinator forms the first cluster by broadcasting beacon frames; a device that hears a beacon requests to join, becomes a child, then itself begins beaconing so further devices can join through it — building a tree. Higher layers perform the actual formation and routing.
blocks.zh: 网络是如何诞生的？在星形网络组建中（第 5.4.2 条），设备一经激活即可建立自己的网络并成为 PAN 协调器：它选取一个在射程内尚未被占用的 PAN ID，然后允许其他设备加入。在对等网络组建中（第 5.4.3 条），由一个设备被推举为 PAN 协调器——例如最先在信道上发送的那个。簇树是对等拓扑的一个特例：PAN 协调器通过广播信标帧组建第一个簇；听到信标的设备请求加入、成为子节点，随后自己也开始发送信标，使更多设备可经由它加入——从而构建出一棵树。实际的组网与路由由更高层完成。

blocks.en: Architecturally, 802.15.4 is organized into layers, each responsible for one part of the standard and offering services to the layer above (Clause 5.5.1). A device contains at least one PHY — the RF transceiver plus its low-level control — and a MAC sublayer that grants access to the physical channel. The PHY's features (Clause 5.5.2) include turning the radio on and off, energy detection (ED), link quality indication (LQI), channel selection, clear channel assessment (CCA), and transmitting/receiving packets; the HRP UWB PHY adds precision ranging. The MAC sublayer (Clause 5.5.3) offers two services: a data service (moving MPDUs across the PHY) and a management service reached through the MLME-SAP. Its features include beacon management, channel access, GTS management, frame validation, acknowledged delivery, association, and disassociation, plus hooks for security.
blocks.zh: 在架构上，802.15.4 被组织成若干层，每层负责标准的一部分，并向上层提供服务（第 5.5.1 条）。一个设备至少包含一个 PHY——即射频收发机及其底层控制——以及一个用于授予物理信道访问权的 MAC 子层。PHY 的功能（第 5.5.2 条）包括开关无线电、能量检测（ED）、链路质量指示（LQI）、信道选择、空闲信道评估（CCA），以及收发分组；HRP UWB PHY 还增加了精确测距。MAC 子层（第 5.5.3 条）提供两类服务：数据服务（在 PHY 之上搬运 MPDU）与经由 MLME-SAP 访问的管理服务。其功能包括信标管理、信道访问、GTS 管理、帧校验、确认投递、关联与去关联，并为安全机制预留接口。

blocks.en:
flowchart TD
  H[Next higher layers] -->|MCPS-SAP / MLME-SAP| M[MAC sublayer]
  M -->|PD-SAP / PLME-SAP| P[PHY]
  P --> R[Physical medium]
blocks.zh:
flowchart TD
  H[更高层] -->|MCPS-SAP / MLME-SAP| M[MAC 子层]
  M -->|PD-SAP / PLME-SAP| P[PHY 物理层]
  P --> R[物理介质]

blocks.en: The functional overview (Clause 5.6) sketches how the MAC actually works. Scheduled access (5.6.1) offers several superframe structures — beacon, DSME, TSCH slotframes, and TMCTP — for time-organized communication. The data transfer model (5.6.2) has three flavors: to a coordinator, indirect from a coordinator (the coordinator flags pending data in its beacon and the device polls for it with a Data Request command), and peer-to-peer; correctly received frames that request it get an acknowledgment (Imm-Ack, Enh-Ack, or Frak). The frame structure (5.6.3) is kept minimal yet robust: a MAC frame becomes the PHY payload (PSDU) inside a PPDU made of a synchronization header (SHR), PHY header (PHR), and payload, with a MAC header (MHR) and MAC footer (MFR) around the MAC payload. Information elements (5.6.4) are typed, length-prefixed chunks of formatted data that a device can read or skip if unknown.
blocks.zh: 功能概述（第 5.6 条）勾勒了 MAC 的实际运作方式。调度访问（5.6.1）提供多种超帧结构——信标超帧、DSME、TSCH 时隙帧以及 TMCTP——用于按时间组织通信。数据传输模型（5.6.2）有三种形式：发往协调器、由协调器间接发出（协调器在其信标中标记有待发数据，设备用数据请求命令轮询取回），以及对等传输；被正确接收且要求确认的帧会收到一个确认（Imm-Ack、Enh-Ack 或 Frak）。帧结构（5.6.3）力求精简而稳健：一个 MAC 帧成为 PPDU 内部的 PHY 负载（PSDU），PPDU 由同步头（SHR）、PHY 头（PHR）与负载组成，MAC 负载外侧则包裹 MAC 头（MHR）与 MAC 尾（MFR）。信息元素（5.6.4）是带类型、带长度前缀的格式化数据块，设备可读取它，遇到未知 IE 则跳过。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 440 120" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><text x="220" y="14" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">PPDU</text><rect x="10" y="24" width="90" height="30"/><text x="55" y="43" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">SHR</text><rect x="100" y="24" width="70" height="30"/><text x="135" y="43" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">PHR</text><rect x="170" y="24" width="260" height="30"/><text x="300" y="43" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">PHY payload (PSDU)</text><rect x="170" y="66" width="70" height="30"/><text x="205" y="85" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">MHR</text><rect x="240" y="66" width="120" height="30"/><text x="300" y="85" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">MAC payload</text><rect x="360" y="66" width="70" height="30"/><text x="395" y="85" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">MFR</text><line x1="170" y1="54" x2="170" y2="66" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="430" y1="54" x2="430" y2="66" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/></svg>

caption.en: The MAC frame (MHR + MAC payload + MFR) rides as the PSDU inside the PPDU, behind the PHY's SHR and PHR (Clause 5.6.3).
caption.zh: MAC 帧（MHR + MAC 负载 + MFR）作为 PSDU 装入 PPDU，位于 PHY 的 SHR 与 PHR 之后（第 5.6.3 条）。

summary.en: Coverage map: access methods, power saving, security, spectrum measurement, and primitives
summary.zh: 覆盖地图：访问方法、省电、安全、频谱测量与原语

blocks.en: The remaining functional-overview topics complete the picture. Access methods (5.6.5): the standard defines several channel-sharing schemes — unslotted and slotted CSMA-CA, TSCH CCA and TSCH CSMA-CA, CSMA-CA with priority channel access (PCA), and LECIM ALOHA with PCA (5.6.5.1); a received-and-validated frame is optionally confirmed by an acknowledgment, and an unacknowledged frame is retried (5.6.5.2); the Frak reports which fragments arrived during a fragment sequence (5.6.5.3); and a CRC plus per-fragment FICS verify data integrity (5.6.5.4). Power consumption (5.6.6): the protocol favors battery devices that duty-cycle — sleeping most of the time and waking periodically to check for pending messages, trading battery against latency (5.6.6.1) — with low-energy mechanisms CSL (coordinated sampled listening) and RIT (receiver initiated transmission) cutting energy further (5.6.6.2). Security (5.6.7): symmetric-key cryptography using keys supplied by higher layers provides data confidentiality, data authenticity, and replay protection on a per-frame basis, with link keys (two devices) or group keys; key establishment is out of scope. Spectrum resource measurement (5.6.8): SRM lets devices gather radio-link metrics — locally or by reporting toward the PAN coordinator — to pick the best PAN and use spectrum efficiently, with full, channel-specific, and link-specific measurement modes under TSCH. Finally, the concept of primitives (5.7): a layer's services are described as an information flow of service primitives passed through a SAP, of four generic types — request (initiate a service), indication (signal an internal event), response (complete a procedure begun by an indication), and confirm (convey the results of earlier requests).
blocks.zh: 功能概述的其余主题补全了全貌。访问方法（5.6.5）：标准定义了多种信道共享方案——非时隙与时隙 CSMA-CA、TSCH CCA 与 TSCH CSMA-CA、带优先信道访问（PCA）的 CSMA-CA，以及带 PCA 的 LECIM ALOHA（5.6.5.1）；被正确接收并校验的帧可选地以确认回应，未确认的帧会被重传（5.6.5.2）；Frak 在分片序列中报告哪些分片已到达（5.6.5.3）；CRC 加上逐分片的 FICS 用于校验数据完整性（5.6.5.4）。功耗考量（5.6.6）：协议偏向采用占空比的电池设备——大部分时间休眠，周期性醒来检查是否有待收消息，以电量换时延（5.6.6.1）——并以低能耗机制 CSL（协调采样侦听）与 RIT（接收方发起的传输）进一步降低能耗（5.6.6.2）。安全（5.6.7）：使用由更高层提供密钥的对称密钥密码学，逐帧提供数据机密性、数据真实性与重放保护，可用链路密钥（两设备）或组密钥；密钥建立不在本标准范围内。频谱资源测量（5.6.8）：SRM 让设备采集无线链路指标——可本地测量或向 PAN 协调器上报——以选择最佳 PAN 并高效利用频谱，在 TSCH 下支持全量、按信道与按链路三种测量模式。最后是原语的概念（5.7）：一层的服务被描述为经由 SAP 传递的服务原语的信息流，共四种通用类型——请求（request，发起服务）、指示（indication，报告内部事件）、响应（response，完成由指示发起的过程）与确认（confirm，传达先前请求的结果）。

blocks.en:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as Service user (N layer)
  participant P as Service provider
  U->>P: Request
  P-->>U: Indication
  U->>P: Response
  P-->>U: Confirm
blocks.zh:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as 服务用户(N 层)
  participant P as 服务提供者
  U->>P: 请求 Request
  P-->>U: 指示 Indication
  U->>P: 响应 Response
  P-->>U: 确认 Confirm

cites.note: Clause 3.1 Definitions and 3.2 Acronyms and abbreviations
Clause 5.1 Introduction, 5.2 Special application spaces (RFID/MBAN/CMB), 5.3 Components
Clause 5.4 Network topologies (star, peer-to-peer, cluster tree)
Clause 5.6 Functional overview and 5.7 Concept of primitives
